<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_options--upi"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_options.upi`

Optional:

- `mandate_options` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--payment_method_options--upi--mandate_options))
- `setup_future_usage` (String) Indicates that you intend to make future payments with this PaymentIntent's payment method.

If you provide a Customer with the PaymentIntent, you can use this parameter to [attach the payment method](/payments/save-during-payment) to the Customer after the PaymentIntent is confirmed and the customer completes any required actions. If you don't provide a Customer, you can still [attach](/api/payment_methods/attach) the payment method to a Customer after the transaction completes.

If the payment method is `card_present` and isn't a digital wallet, Stripe creates and attaches a [generated_card](/api/charges/object#charge_object-payment_method_details-card_present-generated_card) payment method representing the card to the Customer instead.

When processing card payments, Stripe uses `setup_future_usage` to help you comply with regional legislation and network rules, such as [SCA](/strong-customer-authentication).

<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_options--upi--mandate_options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_options.upi.mandate_options`

Optional:

- `amount` (Number) Amount to be charged for future payments.
- `amount_type` (String) One of `fixed` or `maximum`. If `fixed`, the `amount` param refers to the exact amount to be charged in future payments. If `maximum`, the amount charged can be up to the value passed for the `amount` param.
- `description` (String) A description of the mandate or subscription that is meant to be displayed to the customer.
- `end_date` (Number) End date of the mandate or subscription.



<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_options--us_bank_account"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_options.us_bank_account`

Optional:

- `financial_connections` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--payment_method_options--us_bank_account--financial_connections))
- `setup_future_usage` (String) Indicates that you intend to make future payments with this PaymentIntent's payment method.

If you provide a Customer with the PaymentIntent, you can use this parameter to [attach the payment method](/payments/save-during-payment) to the Customer after the PaymentIntent is confirmed and the customer completes any required actions. If you don't provide a Customer, you can still [attach](/api/payment_methods/attach) the payment method to a Customer after the transaction completes.

If the payment method is `card_present` and isn't a digital wallet, Stripe creates and attaches a [generated_card](/api/charges/object#charge_object-payment_method_details-card_present-generated_card) payment method representing the card to the Customer instead.

When processing card payments, Stripe uses `setup_future_usage` to help you comply with regional legislation and network rules, such as [SCA](/strong-customer-authentication).
- `target_date` (String) Controls when Stripe will attempt to debit the funds from the customer's account. The date must be a string in YYYY-MM-DD format. The date must be in the future and between 3 and 15 calendar days from now.
- `verification_method` (String) Bank account verification method. The default value is `automatic`.

<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_options--us_bank_account--financial_connections"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_options.us_bank_account.financial_connections`

Optional:

- `permissions` (List of String) The list of permissions to request. The `payment_method` permission must be included.
- `prefetch` (List of String) Data features requested to be retrieved upon account creation.

Read-Only:

- `filters` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--payment_method_options--us_bank_account--financial_connections--filters))
- `return_url` (String) For webview integrations only. Upon completing OAuth login in the native browser, the user will be redirected to this URL to return to your app.

<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_options--us_bank_account--financial_connections--filters"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_options.us_bank_account.financial_connections.filters`

Read-Only:

- `account_subcategories` (List of String) The account subcategories to use to filter for possible accounts to link. Valid subcategories are `checking` and `savings`.




<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_options--wechat_pay"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_options.wechat_pay`

Required:

- `client` (String) The client type that the end customer will pay from

Optional:

- `app_id` (String) The app ID registered with WeChat Pay. Only required when client is ios or android.
- `setup_future_usage` (String) Indicates that you intend to make future payments with this PaymentIntent's payment method.

If you provide a Customer with the PaymentIntent, you can use this parameter to [attach the payment method](/payments/save-during-payment) to the Customer after the PaymentIntent is confirmed and the customer completes any required actions. If you don't provide a Customer, you can still [attach](/api/payment_methods/attach) the payment method to a Customer after the transaction completes.

If the payment method is `card_present` and isn't a digital wallet, Stripe creates and attaches a [generated_card](/api/charges/object#charge_object-payment_method_details-card_present-generated_card) payment method representing the card to the Customer instead.

When processing card payments, Stripe uses `setup_future_usage` to help you comply with regional legislation and network rules, such as [SCA](/strong-customer-authentication).



<a id="nestedatt--permissions"></a>
### Nested Schema for `permissions`

Optional:

- `update_shipping_details` (String) Determines which entity is allowed to update the shipping details.

Default is `client_only`. Stripe Checkout client will automatically update the shipping details. If set to `server_only`, only your server is allowed to update the shipping details.

When set to `server_only`, you must add the onShippingDetailsChange event handler when initializing the Stripe Checkout client and manually update the shipping details from your server using the Stripe API.


<a id="nestedatt--phone_number_collection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `phone_number_collection`

Required:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Indicates whether phone number collection is enabled for the session


<a id="nestedatt--saved_payment_method_options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `saved_payment_method_options`

Optional:

- `allow_redisplay_filters` (List of String) Uses the `allow_redisplay` value of each saved payment method to filter the set presented to a returning customer. By default, only saved payment methods with ’allow_redisplay: ‘always’ are shown in Checkout.
- `payment_method_remove` (String) Enable customers to choose if they wish to remove their saved payment methods. Disabled by default.
- `payment_method_save` (String) Enable customers to choose if they wish to save their payment method for future use. Disabled by default.


<a id="nestedatt--setup_intent_data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `setup_intent_data`

Optional:

- `description` (String) An arbitrary string attached to the object. Often useful for displaying to users.
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Individual keys can be unset by posting an empty value to them. All keys can be unset by posting an empty value to `metadata`.
- `on_behalf_of` (String) The Stripe account for which the setup is intended.


<a id="nestedatt--shipping_address_collection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_address_collection`

Required:

- `allowed_countries` (List of String) An array of two-letter ISO country codes representing which countries Checkout should provide as options for
shipping locations. Unsupported country codes: `AS, CX, CC, CU, HM, IR, KP, MH, FM, NF, MP, PW, SY, UM, VI`.


<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options`

Optional:

- `shipping_rate` (String) The shipping rate.
- `shipping_rate_data` (Attributes) Parameters to be passed to Shipping Rate creation for this shipping option. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data))

Read-Only:

- `shipping_amount` (Number) A non-negative integer in cents representing how much to charge.

<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options.shipping_rate_data`

Required:

- `display_name` (String) The name of the shipping rate, meant to be displayable to the customer. This will appear on CheckoutSessions.

Optional:

- `delivery_estimate` (Attributes) The estimated range for how long shipping will take, meant to be displayable to the customer. This will appear on CheckoutSessions. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--delivery_estimate))
- `fixed_amount` (Attributes) Describes a fixed amount to charge for shipping. Must be present if type is `fixed_amount`. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--fixed_amount))
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Individual keys can be unset by posting an empty value to them. All keys can be unset by posting an empty value to `metadata`.
- `tax_behavior` (String) Specifies whether the rate is considered inclusive of taxes or exclusive of taxes. One of `inclusive`, `exclusive`, or `unspecified`.
- `tax_code` (String) A [tax code](https://docs.stripe.com/tax/tax-categories) ID. The Shipping tax code is `txcd_92010001`.
- `type` (String) The type of calculation to use on the shipping rate.

<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--delivery_estimate"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options.shipping_rate_data.delivery_estimate`

Optional:

- `maximum` (Attributes) The upper bound of the estimated range. If empty, represents no upper bound i.e., infinite. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--delivery_estimate--maximum))
- `minimum` (Attributes) The lower bound of the estimated range. If empty, represents no lower bound. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--delivery_estimate--minimum))

<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--delivery_estimate--maximum"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options.shipping_rate_data.delivery_estimate.maximum`

Required:

- `unit` (String) A unit of time.
- `value` (Number) Must be greater than 0.


<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--delivery_estimate--minimum"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options.shipping_rate_data.delivery_estimate.minimum`

Required:

- `unit` (String) A unit of time.
- `value` (Number) Must be greater than 0.



<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--fixed_amount"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options.shipping_rate_data.fixed_amount`

Required:

- `amount` (Number) A non-negative integer in cents representing how much to charge.
- `currency` (String) Three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html), in lowercase. Must be a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies).

Optional:

- `currency_options` (Attributes Map) Shipping rates defined in each available currency option. Each key must be a three-letter [ISO currency code](https://www.iso.org/iso-4217-currency-codes.html) and a [supported currency](https://stripe.com/docs/currencies). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--fixed_amount--currency_options))

<a id="nestedatt--shipping_options--shipping_rate_data--fixed_amount--currency_options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_options.shipping_rate_data.fixed_amount.currency_options`

Required:

- `amount` (Number) A non-negative integer in cents representing how much to charge.

Optional:

- `tax_behavior` (String) Specifies whether the rate is considered inclusive of taxes or exclusive of taxes. One of `inclusive`, `exclusive`, or `unspecified`.





<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data`

Optional:

- `application_fee_percent` (Number) A non-negative decimal between 0 and 100, with at most two decimal places. This represents the percentage of the subscription invoice total that will be transferred to the application owner's Stripe account. To use an application fee percent, the request must be made on behalf of another account, using the `Stripe-Account` header or an OAuth key. For more information, see the application fees [documentation](https://stripe.com/docs/connect/subscriptions#collecting-fees-on-subscriptions).
- `billing_cycle_anchor` (Number) A future timestamp to anchor the subscription's billing cycle for new subscriptions. You can't set this parameter if `ui_mode` is `elements`.
- `billing_mode` (Attributes) Controls how prorations and invoices for subscriptions are calculated and orchestrated. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--billing_mode))
- `default_tax_rates` (List of String) The tax rates that will apply to any subscription item that does not have
`tax_rates` set. Invoices created will have their `default_tax_rates` populated
from the subscription.
- `description` (String) The subscription's description, meant to be displayable to the customer.
Use this field to optionally store an explanation of the subscription
for rendering in the [customer portal](https://docs.stripe.com/customer-management).
- `invoice_settings` (Attributes) All invoices will be billed using the specified settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--invoice_settings))
- `metadata` (Map of String) Set of [key-value pairs](https://docs.stripe.com/api/metadata) that you can attach to an object. This can be useful for storing additional information about the object in a structured format. Individual keys can be unset by posting an empty value to them. All keys can be unset by posting an empty value to `metadata`.
- `on_behalf_of` (String) The account on behalf of which to charge, for each of the subscription's invoices.
- `pending_invoice_item_interval` (Attributes) Specifies an interval for how often to bill for any pending invoice items. It is analogous to calling [Create an invoice](https://docs.stripe.com/api#create_invoice) for the given subscription at the specified interval. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--pending_invoice_item_interval))
- `proration_behavior` (String) Determines how to handle prorations resulting from the `billing_cycle_anchor`. If no value is passed, the default is `create_prorations`.
- `transfer_data` (Attributes) If specified, the funds from the subscription's invoices will be transferred to the destination and the ID of the resulting transfers will be found on the resulting charges. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--transfer_data))
- `trial_end` (Number) Unix timestamp representing the end of the trial period the customer will get before being charged for the first time. Has to be at least 48 hours in the future.
- `trial_period_days` (Number) Integer representing the number of trial period days before the customer is charged for the first time. Has to be at least 1.
- `trial_settings` (Attributes) Settings related to subscription trials. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--trial_settings))

<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--billing_mode"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.billing_mode`

Required:

- `type` (String) Controls the calculation and orchestration of prorations and invoices for subscriptions. If no value is passed, the default is `flexible`.

Optional:

- `flexible` (Attributes) Configure behavior for flexible billing mode. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--billing_mode--flexible))

<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--billing_mode--flexible"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.billing_mode.flexible`

Optional:

- `proration_discounts` (String) Controls how invoices and invoice items display proration amounts and discount amounts.



<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--invoice_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.invoice_settings`

Optional:

- `issuer` (Attributes) The connected account that issues the invoice. The invoice is presented with the branding and support information of the specified account. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--invoice_settings--issuer))

<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--invoice_settings--issuer"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.invoice_settings.issuer`

Required:

- `type` (String) Type of the account referenced in the request.

Optional:

- `account` (String) The connected account being referenced when `type` is `account`.



<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--pending_invoice_item_interval"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.pending_invoice_item_interval`

Required:

- `interval` (String) Specifies invoicing frequency. Either `day`, `week`, `month` or `year`.

Optional:

- `interval_count` (Number) The number of intervals between invoices. For example, `interval=month` and `interval_count=3` bills every 3 months. Maximum of one year interval allowed (1 year, 12 months, or 52 weeks).


<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--transfer_data"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.transfer_data`

Required:

- `destination` (String) ID of an existing, connected Stripe account.

Optional:

- `amount_percent` (Number) A non-negative decimal between 0 and 100, with at most two decimal places. This represents the percentage of the subscription invoice total that will be transferred to the destination account. By default, the entire amount is transferred to the destination.


<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--trial_settings"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.trial_settings`

Required:

- `end_behavior` (Attributes) Defines how the subscription should behave when the user's free trial ends. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--subscription_data--trial_settings--end_behavior))

<a id="nestedatt--subscription_data--trial_settings--end_behavior"></a>
### Nested Schema for `subscription_data.trial_settings.end_behavior`

Required:

- `missing_payment_method` (String) Indicates how the subscription should change when the trial ends if the user did not provide a payment method.




<a id="nestedatt--tax_id_collection"></a>
### Nested Schema for `tax_id_collection`

Required:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Indicates whether tax ID collection is enabled for the session

Optional:

- `required` (String) Indicates whether a tax ID is required on the payment page


<a id="nestedatt--wallet_options"></a>
### Nested Schema for `wallet_options`

Optional:

- `link` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--wallet_options--link))

<a id="nestedatt--wallet_options--link"></a>
### Nested Schema for `wallet_options.link`

Optional:

- `display` (String) Describes whether Checkout should display Link. Defaults to `auto`.



<a id="nestedatt--consent"></a>
### Nested Schema for `consent`

Read-Only:

- `promotions` (String) If `opt_in`, the customer consents to receiving promotional communications
from the merchant about this Checkout Session.
- `terms_of_service` (String) If `accepted`, the customer in this Checkout Session has agreed to the merchant's terms of service.


<a id="nestedatt--currency_conversion"></a>
### Nested Schema for `currency_conversion`

Read-Only:

- `amount_subtotal` (Number) Total of all items in source currency before discounts or taxes are applied.
- `amount_total` (Number) Total of all items in source currency after discounts and taxes are applied.
- `fx_rate` (Number) Exchange rate used to convert source currency amounts to customer currency amounts
- `source_currency` (String) Creation currency of the CheckoutSession before localization


<a id="nestedatt--customer_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `customer_details`

Read-Only:

- `address` (Attributes) The customer's address after a completed Checkout Session. Note: This property is populated only for sessions on or after March 30, 2022. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--customer_details--address))
- `business_name` (String) The customer's business name after a completed Checkout Session.
- `email` (String) The email associated with the Customer, if one exists, on the Checkout Session after a completed Checkout Session or at time of session expiry.
Otherwise, if the customer has consented to promotional content, this value is the most recent valid email provided by the customer on the Checkout form.
- `individual_name` (String) The customer's individual name after a completed Checkout Session.
- `name` (String) The customer's name after a completed Checkout Session. Note: This property is populated only for sessions on or after March 30, 2022.
- `phone` (String) The customer's phone number after a completed Checkout Session.
- `tax_exempt` (String) The customer’s tax exempt status after a completed Checkout Session.
- `tax_ids` (Attributes List) The customer’s tax IDs after a completed Checkout Session. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--customer_details--tax_ids))

<a id="nestedatt--customer_details--address"></a>
### Nested Schema for `customer_details.address`

Read-Only:

- `city` (String) City, district, suburb, town, or village.
- `country` (String) Two-letter country code ([ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2)).
- `line1` (String) Address line 1, such as the street, PO Box, or company name.
- `line2` (String) Address line 2, such as the apartment, suite, unit, or building.
- `postal_code` (String) ZIP or postal code.
- `state` (String) State, county, province, or region ([ISO 3166-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-2)).


<a id="nestedatt--customer_details--tax_ids"></a>
### Nested Schema for `customer_details.tax_ids`

Read-Only:

- `type` (String) The type of the tax ID, one of `ad_nrt`, `ar_cuit`, `eu_vat`, `bo_tin`, `br_cnpj`, `br_cpf`, `cn_tin`, `co_nit`, `cr_tin`, `do_rcn`, `ec_ruc`, `eu_oss_vat`, `hr_oib`, `pe_ruc`, `ro_tin`, `rs_pib`, `sv_nit`, `uy_ruc`, `ve_rif`, `vn_tin`, `gb_vat`, `nz_gst`, `au_abn`, `au_arn`, `in_gst`, `no_vat`, `no_voec`, `za_vat`, `ch_vat`, `mx_rfc`, `sg_uen`, `ru_inn`, `ru_kpp`, `ca_bn`, `hk_br`, `es_cif`, `pl_nip`, `it_cf`, `fo_vat`, `gi_tin`, `py_ruc`, `tw_vat`, `th_vat`, `jp_cn`, `jp_rn`, `jp_trn`, `li_uid`, `li_vat`, `lk_vat`, `my_itn`, `us_ein`, `kr_brn`, `ca_qst`, `ca_gst_hst`, `ca_pst_bc`, `ca_pst_mb`, `ca_pst_sk`, `my_sst`, `sg_gst`, `ae_trn`, `cl_tin`, `sa_vat`, `id_npwp`, `my_frp`, `il_vat`, `ge_vat`, `ua_vat`, `is_vat`, `bg_uic`, `hu_tin`, `si_tin`, `ke_pin`, `tr_tin`, `eg_tin`, `ph_tin`, `al_tin`, `bh_vat`, `kz_bin`, `ng_tin`, `om_vat`, `de_stn`, `ch_uid`, `tz_vat`, `uz_vat`, `uz_tin`, `md_vat`, `ma_vat`, `by_tin`, `ao_tin`, `bs_tin`, `bb_tin`, `cd_nif`, `mr_nif`, `me_pib`, `zw_tin`, `ba_tin`, `gn_nif`, `mk_vat`, `sr_fin`, `sn_ninea`, `am_tin`, `np_pan`, `tj_tin`, `ug_tin`, `zm_tin`, `kh_tin`, `aw_tin`, `az_tin`, `bd_bin`, `bj_ifu`, `et_tin`, `kg_tin`, `la_tin`, `cm_niu`, `cv_nif`, `bf_ifu`, or `unknown`
- `value` (String) The value of the tax ID.



<a id="nestedatt--payment_method_configuration_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `payment_method_configuration_details`

Read-Only:

- `id` (String) ID of the payment method configuration used.
- `parent` (String) ID of the parent payment method configuration used.


<a id="nestedatt--presentment_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `presentment_details`

Read-Only:

- `presentment_amount` (Number) Amount intended to be collected by this payment, denominated in `presentment_currency`.
- `presentment_currency` (String) Currency presented to the customer during payment.


<a id="nestedatt--shipping_cost"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_cost`

Read-Only:

- `amount_subtotal` (Number) Total shipping cost before any discounts or taxes are applied.
- `amount_tax` (Number) Total tax amount applied due to shipping costs. If no tax was applied, defaults to 0.
- `amount_total` (Number) Total shipping cost after discounts and taxes are applied.
- `shipping_rate` (String) The ID of the ShippingRate for this order.
- `taxes` (Attributes List) The taxes applied to the shipping rate. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--shipping_cost--taxes))

<a id="nestedatt--shipping_cost--taxes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `shipping_cost.taxes`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) Amount of tax applied for this rate.
- `rate` (String) Tax rates can be applied to [invoices](/invoicing/taxes/tax-rates), [subscriptions](/billing/taxes/tax-rates) and [Checkout Sessions](/payments/checkout/use-manual-tax-rates) to collect tax.

Related guide: [Tax rates](/billing/taxes/tax-rates)
- `taxability_reason` (String) The reasoning behind this tax, for example, if the product is tax exempt. The possible values for this field may be extended as new tax rules are supported.
- `taxable_amount` (Number) The amount on which tax is calculated, in cents (or local equivalent).



<a id="nestedatt--total_details"></a>
### Nested Schema for `total_details`

Read-Only:

- `amount_discount` (Number) This is the sum of all the discounts.
- `amount_shipping` (Number) This is the sum of all the shipping amounts.
- `amount_tax` (Number) This is the sum of all the tax amounts.
- `breakdown` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--total_details--breakdown))

<a id="nestedatt--total_details--breakdown"></a>
### Nested Schema for `total_details.breakdown`

Read-Only:

- `discounts` (Attributes List) The aggregated discounts. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--total_details--breakdown--discounts))
- `taxes` (Attributes List) The aggregated tax amounts by rate. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--total_details--breakdown--taxes))

<a id="nestedatt--total_details--breakdown--discounts"></a>
### Nested Schema for `total_details.breakdown.discounts`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) The amount discounted.
- `discount` (String) A discount represents the actual application of a [coupon](https://api.stripe.com#coupons) or [promotion code](https://api.stripe.com#promotion_codes).
It contains information about when the discount began, when it will end, and what it is applied to.

Related guide: [Applying discounts to subscriptions](https://docs.stripe.com/billing/subscriptions/discounts)


<a id="nestedatt--total_details--breakdown--taxes"></a>
### Nested Schema for `total_details.breakdown.taxes`

Read-Only:

- `amount` (Number) Amount of tax applied for this rate.
- `rate` (String) Tax rates can be applied to [invoices](/invoicing/taxes/tax-rates), [subscriptions](/billing/taxes/tax-rates) and [Checkout Sessions](/payments/checkout/use-manual-tax-rates) to collect tax.

Related guide: [Tax rates](/billing/taxes/tax-rates)
- `taxability_reason` (String) The reasoning behind this tax, for example, if the product is tax exempt. The possible values for this field may be extended as new tax rules are supported.
- `taxable_amount` (Number) The amount on which tax is calculated, in cents (or local equivalent).
